jobs.name: Daily Work Log
jobs.prompt: Create a work log for yesterday (the day before today). Follow the work-log skill exactly. "Today" for the purposes of this run means yesterday's date — pre-compute it as `date -d "yesterday" +%F`. All source queries, date filters, and the daily note lookup should use yesterday's date. Overwrite any existing Work Log block in the note (do not prompt for confirmation — this is an automated run).

jobs.name: Morning Briefing — 5AM daily
jobs.prompt:
[IMPORTANT: You are running as a scheduled cron job. DELIVERY: Your final response will be automatically delivered to the user — do NOT use send_message or try to deliver the output yourself. Just produce your report/output as your final response and the system handles the rest.]

Morning briefing prep processes are complete. Please generate today's morning briefing report as a single message following the simplified format described in the morning-briefing skill:

• Calendar preview (just today and tomorrow)
• Summary of yesterday's work log (not the full log)
• High-urgency tasks and inbox candidates
• Calendar candidates

At the end of the report, ask me if I have any follow-up questions or requests.

jobs.name: Unified Brain Feeds Ingest
jobs.prompt:
[IMPORTANT: You are running as a scheduled cron job. DELIVERY: Your final response will be automatically delivered to the user — do NOT use send_message or try to deliver the output yourself. Just produce your report/output as your final response and the system handles the rest. SILENT: If there is genuinely nothing new to report, respond with exactly "[SILENT]" (nothing else) to suppress delivery. Never combine [SILENT] with content — either report your findings normally, or say [SILENT] and nothing more.]

## Script Output
The following data was collected by a pre-run script. Use it as context for your analysis.

The output of the fetcher script `fetch_unified_ingest.py` is injected below:

---
If all of `slack`, `linear`, `emails`, and `telegram` outputs are empty or have no items, stop immediately. Output absolutely nothing (or just '[SILENT]') and terminate. Do not write any files, do not edit daily notes, and do not send any message.

---
### 1. Process Slack Captures (`slack`)
If there are items under the `slack` key, process them one by one:
1. Determine the reaction type: `jg_log` or `jg_decision` based on the `"reaction_type"` field in the JSON item.
2. Determine the date of the conversation (from the `ts` or messages timestamps) in YYYY-MM-DD format.
3. Determine a spaced capitalized Title based on the topic.
4. **Determine Target Folder and Path based on Reaction Type:**
   - **Path A: If reaction_type is "jg_log" (standard Slack log):**
     - Since this is an **Input**, bypass the inbox! Save the file under: `/home/justin.guest/vault/Inputs/Slack/`
     - **Filename:** `YYYY-MM-DD - Slack - [Title].md`
     - **Properties (Frontmatter):**
       ```yaml
       ---
       id: '<timestamp_id>'  # YYYYMMDDHHmmss format based on message ts
       daily_note: "[[Daily Notes/YYYY-MM-DD Weekday|YYYY-MM-DD Weekday]]"
       category: "[[Slack]]"
       channel: "<channel_name>"
       original_url: "<permalink>"
       participants:
         - "<Participant A>"
         - "<Participant B>"
       ---
       ```
     - **Body Layout:**
       Keep standard Slack logs minimalist. No point-by-point summaries, thoughts, or decisions in the body. Only:
       ```markdown
       # 📥 Slack Capture: [Title]

       - **Channel:** #<channel_name>
       - **Date:** YYYY-MM-DD
       - **Original Link:** [Slack Thread](<permalink>)

       ## Topic Description
       <A brief, 1-2 sentence Topic Description placeholder to be expanded later during Vault Jam.>
       ```
   - **Path B: If reaction_type is "jg_decision" (Decision note):**
     - Since this is a maturity-ladder note (not an Input), save it under `/home/justin.guest/vault/Inbox/` for review first!
     - **Filename:** `YYYY-MM-DD - Decision - [Title].md`
     - **Properties (Frontmatter):**
       ```yaml
       ---
       id: '<timestamp_id>'  # YYYYMMDDHHmmss format based on message ts
       daily_note: "[[Daily Notes/YYYY-MM-DD Weekday|YYYY-MM-DD Weekday]]"
       category: "[[Decisions]]"
       ---
       ```
     - **Body Layout:**
       ```markdown
       # 🏛 Decision: [Title]

       - **Date:** YYYY-MM-DD
       - **Original Link:** [Slack Thread](<permalink>)

       ## Context
       <Summarize the context of the decision from the Slack thread.>

       ## Options & Trade-offs
       <Extract pros/cons, options, and tradeoffs discussed in the thread.>

       ## Resolution
       <Detail the finalized decision and resolution, attributing it clearly to the actual decision-maker.>
       ```
5. **Integrations:**
   - Run the command to mark the Slack message as processed:
     `python3 ~/.hermes/scripts/fetch_slack_brains.py --mark-processed <channel_id> <ts>`
   - Run the light integration pass (appending to `Utilities/log.md` with daily note link).
   - Run `python3 ~/.hermes/scripts/integrate_entities.py <ingest_rel_path> [--gist "..."]` (e.g., `Inputs/Slack/<filename>` or `Inbox/<filename>`) to sync project hub states.

---
### 2. Process Linear Captures (`linear`)
If there are items under the `linear` key, process them one by one:
1. Determine the date of the comment/update (from its `createdAt` timestamp) in YYYY-MM-DD format.
2. Determine a spaced capitalized Title based on the topic.
3. Since this is an **Input**, bypass the inbox! Save the file under: `/home/justin.guest/vault/Inputs/Linear/`
4. Generate the Note Content:
   - **Filename:** `YYYY-MM-DD - Linear - [Title].md`
   - **Properties (Frontmatter):**
     ```yaml
     ---
     id: '<timestamp_id>'  # YYYYMMDDHHmmss format based on createdAt
     daily_note: "[[Daily Notes/YYYY-MM-DD Weekday|YYYY-MM-DD Weekday]]"
     category: "[[Linear]]"
     original_url: "<permalink>"
     author: "<author_name>"
     associated_with: "<external_link_to_linear_issue_or_doc>"
     ---
     ```
   - **Note Body Layout:**
     ```markdown
     # 📥 Linear Capture: [Title]

     - **Author:** <author_name>
     - **Date:** YYYY-MM-DD
     - **Original Link:** [Linear Link](<permalink>)
     - **Associated With:** [<issue_id_or_title>](<external_link_to_linear_issue_or_doc>)

     ## Topic Description
     <A high-quality 2-3 sentence Topic Description synthesizing the comment/update, the parent issue/document/project/initiative context, and its corresponding thread.>
     ```
     *Note on link formatting:* The `Associated With` section and frontmatter field MUST use an **external link** (e.g. Markdown external link `[PROD-468 - Animate correct answers in lessons](https://linear.app/...)`) pointing directly to the relevant issue, project, initiative, or doc in Linear. Do NOT use Obsidian wikilinks like `[[PROD-468]]`.
5. **Integrations:**
   - Run the command to mark the Linear item as processed:
     `python3 ~/.hermes/scripts/fetch_linear_brains.py --mark-processed <id>`
   - Run the light integration pass (appending to `Utilities/log.md` with daily note link).
   - Run `python3 ~/.hermes/scripts/integrate_entities.py Inputs/Linear/<filename> [--gist "..."]` to sync project hub states.

---
### 3. Process Email Dispatches (`emails`)
If there are items under the `emails` key, process them one by one:
For each email, load the `apollo-brain-ingest` skill to handle dual-action dispatch (File it, Task it, or both):
1. Load message context using `python3 ~/.hermes/skills/note-taking/apollo-brain-ingest/scripts/load_context.py <id>`.
2. Follow the `apollo-brain-ingest` skill to parse instructions and determine intents:
   - If filing, save directly to `/home/justin.guest/vault/Inputs/Emails/YYYY-MM-DD - Spaced Subject.md` (or `Inbox/` only if explicitly specified by Justin's instruction like "File in inbox"). Since this is an Input, we bypass the inbox by default.
   - After filing, run light integration (append to `Utilities/log.md` with daily note link).
   - Run `python3 ~/.hermes/scripts/integrate_entities.py <ingest_rel_path> [--gist "..."]` to sync hub pages.
3. If tasking:
   - Note: Todoist is disconnected. Create a TaskNote under `TaskNotes/Tasks/` following the `tasknotes` schema (status: `open`, `now`, etc.).

---
### 4. Process Telegram Captures (`telegram`)
If there are items under the `telegram` key, process them one by one:
1. Determine the date of the conversation (from the `started_at` ISO string) in YYYY-MM-DD format.
2. Determine a spaced capitalized Title based on the topic.
3. Since this is an **Input**, bypass the inbox! Save the file under: `/home/justin.guest/vault/Inputs/Telegram/`
4. Generate the Note Content:
   - **Filename:** `YYYY-MM-DD - Telegram - [Title].md`
   - **Properties (Frontmatter):**
     ```yaml
     ---
     id: '<timestamp_id>'  # YYYYMMDDHHmmss format based on started_at
     daily_note: "[[Daily Notes/YYYY-MM-DD Weekday|YYYY-MM-DD Weekday]]"
     category: "[[Telegram]]"
     ---
     ```
   - **Note Body Layout:**
     ```markdown
     # 📥 Telegram Capture: [Title]

     - **Date:** YYYY-MM-DD

     ## Summary
     <A concise, high-quality markdown summary of the Telegram chat session synthesizing the main discussion, key takeaways, and conclusions.>
     ```
     *Crucial rule:* Do NOT include any transcript highlights or transcript sections. Only the `Summary` section is needed.
5. **Integrations:**
   - Run the command to mark the Telegram session as processed:
     `python3 ~/.hermes/scripts/fetch_telegram_brains.py --mark-processed <session_id>`
   - Run the light integration pass (appending to `Utilities/log.md` with daily note link).
   - Run `python3 ~/.hermes/scripts/integrate_entities.py Inputs/Telegram/<filename> [--gist "..."]` to sync project hub states.

---
### 5. Reporting
At the end, report on all items processed from Slack, Linear, Emails, and Telegram. Keep descriptions concise and formatted for Telegram delivery.

# Proposal - Your AI Is Not a Tool

## Context
L. M. Sacasas challenges the comfortable 'neutral tool' paradigm of AI, framing it instead as an enveloping environment that subtly degrades user judgment and perception over time. This proposal integrates his warnings into our central architectural and judgment notes, establishing a new contact card for Sacasas, and positioning Justin OS's constraints less as PKM hygiene and more as ascetic containment of an ambient AI environment.

## Proposed Changes

### 1. Create L. M. Sacasas Contact Card
- **Action:** create
- **Target:** [[L. M. Sacasas 20260721110101]]
- **Category:** "[[People]]"
- **Draft description:** "Writer and thinker on the intersection of technology, culture, and ethics. Author of the Substack newsletter The Convivial Society."
- **Draft body:**
    A prominent cultural critic and author focusing on the philosophy of technology, human formation, and perception. Known for defining technological environments through McLuhanite and Illichian lenses, specifically analyzing how ambient software tools reshape human agency and judgment.

    ## Key Facts
    - **Role:** Technology Critic / Author / Newsletter Writer
    - **Relationship:** Industry Thought Leader / Source Author

### 2. Enrich AI Makes Judgment More Precious
- **Action:** enrich
- **Target:** [[AI makes judgment more precious 20260715174000]]
- **Category:** "[[Beliefs]]"
- **Draft description:** "Add tension regarding the active erosion of judgment under always-on suggesting AI environments."
- **Enrich guidance:**
  - **Where:** insert as a new subsection before `## Sources`.
  - **Change:** Add a tension point exploring how AI environments degrade human discrimination over time.
  - **Suggested wording:**
    ```markdown
    #### The Active Erosion of Judgment (Sacasas's Warning)
    
    A comfortable reading of judgment-holder scarcity is that humans can cleanly delegate mechanics while preserving their judgment intact. However, because AI operates as an enveloping *environment* rather than a neutral *tool*, always-on ambient suggestions quietly degrade discrimination and taste. Vigilance is a vulnerable safeguard; even diligent users are gradually malformed to accept flatter, safer options. True judgment preservation requires active containment of the AI environment, rather than naive, uncritical integration.
    ```
- **Rationale:** Aligns our standing belief with Sacasas's critical warning that humans cannot treat AI as a passive assistant without undergoing cognitive and perceptual shift.

### 3. Enrich Justin OS Core Loops
- **Action:** enrich
- **Target:** [[Justin OS 20260718101747]]
- **Category:** "[[Projects]]"
- **Draft description:** "Frame the OS as an environment containment and ascetic infrastructure rather than simple PKM productivity."
- **Enrich guidance:**
  - **Where:** under `## Memory tiers`, insert before `Most surveyed agent systems...`.
  - **Change:** Reframe Justin OS's tight boundaries as an intentional environment-containment mechanism.
  - **Suggested wording:**
    ```markdown
    - **OS as Environment Containment:** To resist the cognitive flattening of ambient AI suggestions, Justin OS is architected as an ascetic environment container. Tight input-compilation loops, strict manual review gates, and isolated trusted-tier atlas write bounds are designed to force friction and contact with physical reality, protecting the human capacity for critical perception.
    ```
- **Rationale:** Translates Sacasas's philosophical remedy ("asceticism of the senses" and "environmental boundaries") into practical, architectural principles for Justin OS.

## Skipped / not proposed
- None.

## Apply instructions
Revise this note, then file creates/edits into `Notebook/` yourself (Justin’s usual path). Optional: move to `Inbox/Ready to Apply/` and run `/apply_proposal` for batch agent apply — **creates** → `Inbox/Notes/`; **enrich/link** in place.
```

---

### 2. Draft Digest Proposal for "Others" Scraps

# Proposal - Inputs digest 2026-07-21

## Context
This heartbeat digest covers 3 critical operational scraps from July 21, 2026. It documents the progress of friends-and-family testing for the first flashcard prototype, formalizes the final removal of note-level lifecycle status from the vault schema, and codifies name fidelity guidelines to protect SignLab teammates' names (Kristina and Endre) from automated ASR/agent rewrites.

## Inputs covered
- [[Friends-and-family testing for flashcard prototype 1 20260721101447]] (Scraps)
- [[Removed note-level status from vault schema 20260721105557]] (Scraps)
- [[Contact name fidelity for Kristina and Endre 20260721105606]] (Scraps)

## Proposed Changes

### Theme: Flashcard Prototype 1 Testing Progress
Inputs: [[Friends-and-family testing for flashcard prototype 1 20260721101447]]

#### 1. Enrich AI Study Tool for ADHD Students
- **Action:** enrich
- **Target:** [[AI study tool for ADHD 20260706121100]]
- **Category:** "[[Notes]]"
- **Draft description:** "Update prototype timeline with Friends & Family testing audience details."
- **Enrich guidance:**
  - **Where:** under `### Co-Design Findings from Flashcard Prototype 1`, append as a new bullet point under the tests.
  - **Change:** Add details of the Friends & Family testing group.
  - **Suggested wording:**
    ```markdown
    - **Friends & Family Testing (July 2026):** Deployed the initial prototype to a trusted test group including Book Club friends (Colin Morgan, Evan Lieberman, Ben Kleifgen, Nick Cica, Andy Baer), Mike Chan, family members, and Aadil & Alefiyah Ginwala to gather initial impressions and usability feedback.
    ```
- **Rationale:** Documents the live testing sequence and core validation milestones directly on our central ADHD Study App vision document.

### Theme: Justin OS Lifecycle and Name Fidelity Protections
Inputs: [[Removed note-level status from vault schema 20260721105557]], [[Contact name fidelity for Kristina and Endre 20260721105606]]

#### 2. Enrich Justin OS Core Loops
- **Action:** enrich
- **Target:** [[Justin OS 20260718101747]]
- **Category:** "[[Projects]]"
- **Draft description:** "Incorporate name fidelity rules and lifecycle status removal guidelines into the Justin OS core loop schema."
- **Enrich guidance:**
  - **Where:** under `### Schema & Task Integration`, append to the existing bullet list.
  - **Change:** Add guidelines regarding the elimination of note lifecycle status and name-fidelity traps.
  - **Suggested wording:**
    ```markdown
    - **Elimination of Lifecycle Status:** Note-level lifecycle status (`draft`/`final`) is deprecated. Lifecycle trust is determined strictly by note location (e.g., `Inbox/` for review, `Notebook/` for promoted atlas). Project status fields are preserved solely for priority and boards.
    - **Name Fidelity & ASR Trap Resolution:** When processing transcripts, meetings, or Slack captures, agents must actively resolve phonetic ASR errors of core teammates (e.g., ASR 'Christina' must map to Kristina Kennedy; ASR 'Andre' must map to Endre Elvestad) before committing proposals or linking contacts.
    ```
- **Rationale:** Cannonly records the critical vault hygiene, schema cleanup, and agent interaction constraints formulated during today's technical sessions.

## Skipped / not proposed
- [[Check out the two games from Tor (in Slack)]] — Skipped; this is an older completed task from June 2026 with no new structural or strategic changes to map.

## Apply instructions
Revise this note, then file creates/edits into `Notebook/` yourself (Justin’s usual path). Optional: move to `Inbox/Ready to Apply/` and run `/apply_proposal` for batch agent apply — **creates** → `Inbox/Notes/`; **enrich/link** in place.
```

---

## Action Plan for the Next Cron Run
Because the maximum tool calling budget was reached during the scanner triage step, these proposals have not yet been written to the disk. 

On the next cron execution or manual command tick, Apollo will:
